summary: The PoC demonstrates that an attacker-controlled notifyUri stored during charging session creation is used without validation to make an outbound HTTP POST when a recharge notification is triggered, constituting a Server-Side Request Forgery.
expected_security_impact: The CHF server should make an outbound HTTP POST to the attacker-specified URL (http://127.0.0.1:8888/ssrf-proof), proving that the server can be used as an SSRF proxy to reach internal or arbitrary network endpoints.
actual_observed_result: Not yet executed. The reproduction bundle is prepared and self-contained for future rerun.
verification_method: The verify.sh helper starts a TCP listener on port 8888 before triggering the exploit. After the PUT /recharging request, the listener checks whether an inbound HTTP connection was received on the /ssrf-proof path. A successful connection confirms the SSRF; absence of a connection after a 10-second timeout indicates the exploit did not trigger.
limitations_or_blockers: The CHF requires Go toolchain and MongoDB to build and run. The go.mod declares go 1.25.5 which may not be available on all Docker base images; the build script adjusts the version directive.
The CHF requires NRF registration; the server starts anyway but logs an error, which does not block the HTTP endpoints.
The Diameter handlers for rating and ABMF are not needed for the SSRF path, so the exploit works without external Diameter servers.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
